---
title: 'Distortion - Street Noise - Scene One Rewrite (Edited 10/08/16)'
date: '2016-10-24T12:53:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3244173256232013881'
blogger_filename: '/2016/10/distortion-street-noise-scene-one_55.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
---

<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, serif; font-size: 12pt;">A&nbsp;man&nbsp;in<br />black&nbsp;disappeared behind a gray Chevy Suburban across the street. Ash<br />spotted him through the window of The Grounded Café while waiting for his<br />morning Java Chip Frappe'. </span><i style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, serif; font-size: 12pt;">Wait, was he&nbsp;wearing combat gear?</i><br /><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ice shards tore down his spine, his<br />heart skipped a beat. The muscles in his right arm tensed as he reached for the<br />Glock 9mm holstered on&nbsp;his side. He<br />shook his head, on second glance the man&nbsp;was&nbsp;gone, a cloud of charcoal<br />dust whisping into the air from under the<br />SUV.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">A breath slowly escaped his lungs,<br />Ash released his grip on what he originally thought was his Glock 9mm, but he<br />now realized was a holstered steel tape measure. He wasn't licensed to carry<br />anymore, so he typically didn't. He let his arm slowly lower to his side as he<br />closed his eyes. Taking a deliberately slow breath, he inhaled&nbsp;counting to<br />ten, silently. The scents of blonde roast and espresso filled his mind and<br />centered him.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Even after eight years, war long<br />over, he still could not trust what he first sees. Eyes open again, the world<br />returned to normal. No threats. Just passers-by,<br />shoppers on a crowded small town tourist street in Ashland Oregon. A young lady<br />walked&nbsp;by outside, face glued to her screen like so many others. She<br />bumped into an elderly homeless gentlemen<br />but&nbsp;didn't look up, or give any indication to acknowledge the incident. He<br />walked off mumbling to himself. A gray<br />dust cloud&nbsp;hung around her shoulders like a shawl, and riding&nbsp;the<br />cloud was a small green frog with red eyes. Its legs disappeared into the mist,<br />becoming one with it. As she passed, the frog held Ash's gaze; head turning<br />unnaturally backward. He shuddered, no matter how many times he saw them, they<br />were still creepy.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">To the best of his knowledge, nobody<br />else could see the colorful dust clouds that wrapped people like a shawl or<br />overcoat, nor the creatures that accompanied them. Each persons' cloud seemed<br />to be colored by their mood. Not everyone had a pet creature, but many did. This<br />insight&nbsp;was his unique gift,&nbsp;a prize brought home from overseas. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Don't worry so much,"<br />Theron "Ash" Ashland could still hear his&nbsp;doctor's advice from<br />their last session a few weeks back.&nbsp;"We’re just beginning to<br />understand PTSD and many veterans come back with&nbsp;hallucinations. The mind<br />is a marvelous adapter to stress. As long as these phantoms do not interrupt<br />your daily work, or cause you to want to harm yourself or others, you should be<br />fine. Consider yourself lucky. Just think of them as a construct of your<br />over-active imagination, an amusing distraction from the mundane realities of<br />life." The medication seemed to help, a little. The visions were no less<br />common but he felt more detached from them now, a casual observer.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Movement from above, just outside<br />the window caught his attention. The shade of a Basswood tree was crawling down<br />the sidewalk for the days' last stretch. A&nbsp;yellow and orange leaf fell,<br />landing on a coffee table on the&nbsp;empty patio. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Ash,"&nbsp;the Barista<br />called. "One Java Chip. Are you ever going to let me talk you into trying<br />something else?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"I doubt it, Jake, maybe next<br />time," Ash replied and grabbed a coffee sleeve.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"That's what you said last<br />week.&nbsp;You&nbsp;working on&nbsp;anything new lately?"&nbsp;Jake asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash glanced at him,&nbsp;forced a<br />smile, and replied, "Yeah,&nbsp;I've got a few new pieces. Just dropped<br />them off this morning over at Woodmen Furnishings. You should go by later, a<br />few of the other craftsmen were there...&nbsp;lots of new inventory."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Will do," Jake said as he<br />turned back to start another order. "Good to see you, as always."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash said, "You<br />too,"&nbsp;as he headed to a far corner and&nbsp;found his favorite<br />leather chair.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">He glanced around the café before<br />taking a seat. Two men&nbsp;just arrived while he was waiting, they were having<br />a meeting. One with glasses, the other in a black turtleneck. Two women talking about their kids nearby. Three<br />teenagers typing away on laptops as though their life depended on it. <i>Always<br />keep your back to the wall, stay as inconspicuous as possible</i>. The need to<br />be hyper-vigilant was like breathing, it never stopped. With the possible<br />exception of the guy in the turtleneck<br />who looked like he could handle himself, everyone seemed fairly free of danger.<br />He bit his lip and sighed, "You're doing it again..." He took his<br />seat, the leather hugged him like a cool blanket.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The morning paper had been abandoned<br />on a nearby table, as was the&nbsp;café custom. He reached to grab it when he<br />noticed&nbsp;several people outside turning and staring in the same direction.<br />Others ran from across the street to join a&nbsp;crowd that was developing just<br />outside the window. Whatever had their attention was just<br />out&nbsp;of&nbsp;view. He didn't want to get back up, but curiosity got the<br />better of him.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">After he exited the front door,<br />he&nbsp;turned to the crowd.&nbsp;The man&nbsp;with the&nbsp;glasses followed<br />from inside the coffee shop, turtleneck<br />in tow. Turtleneck&nbsp;bumped into<br />him.&nbsp;"Excuse me," the man said flatly. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">A voice from the direction of the crowd's gaze was yelling something he couldn't<br />quite hear. Ash wasn't going to get a good view from here, so he walked around<br />the outside of the crowd, keeping his distance from actually touching anyone.<br />The whisps of colorful dust rose and fell<br />off each person. When they were close together like this the dust seemed to<br />combine, grow even. He avoided getting near it if he could help it. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">A woman in a red hoodie rushed<br />passed him to join the crowd and knocked the coffee out of his hand. The lid<br />popped off, contents&nbsp;spilling down the front of his jeans as it tumbled to<br />its final resting place on the asphalt.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Dang lady, watch it!" He<br />said. She paid no attention, engrossed in whatever was happening just beyond<br />his field of vision. "Whatever," he said, more to himself than her,<br />and worked his way around the crowd.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">When he found a clearing on the<br />opposite side of the crowd, he stepped onto the sidewalk. The elderly man, the<br />one the little girl bumped into earlier, was now standing on the bed of a pickup truck. A tattered brown dress coat,<br />stained with years of unwashed use, hung over his thin skin like a cape. The<br />coat&nbsp;may have been black in a former life. A scruffy white beard hid his gaunt features.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">It was Frank, now that he could get<br />close enough to see him up close. Frank&nbsp;was one of the several train-hopping<br />hobos that made camp on the outskirts of town. He came to pick up his Social<br />Security check every month about this time. The man with the glasses was on the<br />other side of the crowd, staring at his tablet, taking pictures no doubt, <i>tourists</i>.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Frank appeared to be having a<br />conversation,&nbsp;"You always did love Mother best-- I know-- That's<br />why-- You couldn't keep your mouth shut--" A creature was rising up out of<br />the dust on his shoulders, only the top half visible. It bore some resemblance<br />to a sloth but moved more quickly. It<br />rotated&nbsp;from shoulder to shoulder, shouting in each ear,&nbsp;visibly<br />agitated. Ash couldn't hear the creature, he never could, but it<br />was&nbsp;feeding the other side of the conversation.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The hair on his neck rose as he<br />watched it. <i>Could this be me if I&nbsp;stop taking my&nbsp;medication?</i><br />He shuddered at the thought.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">A larger creature&nbsp;rose from the<br />mist. This one had a different quality about it, more tangible. The bottom was<br />still rooted in the dust rising and falling from Frank, but the top of the<br />creature was like that of a man. It had the head of a bull with long horns<br />shooting out to each side and curving upward. The Bull-Man was over nine feet<br />tall, black skin made of charcoal,&nbsp;three white streaks were painted across<br />and down&nbsp;its chest.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The bull creature cocked its head to<br />one side and stared Ash in the eye. Its<br />eyes were pools of red, bulging from their sockets. They&nbsp;pierced him as<br />none of the previous creatures had before. The others seemed more interested in<br />their hosts then Ash, but this one was staring him down. A fog entered his<br />mind, his vision clouded over slightly. The muscles in Ash's jaw tightened, and<br />he took a step back. A tingle ran down his thighs.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The man-bull's&nbsp;mouth<br />curved upward, eyes narrowed. It slapped the smaller creature into stillness.<br />Frank looked confused at the sudden silence. The larger creature leaned down<br />and spoke something into Franks' ear. The creature stood upright, nodded to the smaller creature, and<br />they both evaporated back into the dust cloud. Frank turned to look at Ash,<br />eyes narrowed like the man-bull had before. The truck bed gave&nbsp;a rusty<br />creak as Frank leapt off to the ground, spry for an otherwise elderly man. Head tilted<br />awkwardly to one side,&nbsp;he ran at Ash with a wild deranged look in his eye.<br /><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash tried to step out of the way, or<br />run, but his legs were now glued to the<br />pavement. He raised his hands to prepare for a fight, but Frank jumped at him<br />before he could properly react, they both went tumbling to the sidewalk.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash's head slammed into the concrete<br />sending his vision swirling with&nbsp;white dots, and a throbbing throughout<br />his skull. He could feel Franks' hands on his neck, tearing into the flesh.<br />Making a fist, he aimed a punch at Frank through the white dots, where he<br />figured his head would be.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Frank released his grip with one hand and loosened the other.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash caught his breath.<br />"Jesus," he&nbsp;cried as he recoiled at the throbbing in his throat<br />and gagging on the scent of unwashed clothing.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Frank backed up, and looked<br />confused, as though he didn't know where he was. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash took the opening,&nbsp;kicked<br />Frank in the chest and sent him sprawling backward. Losing no momentum, he raced over&nbsp;and punched him&nbsp;with a<br />right hook to the jaw. Frank spun back and fell to the sidewalk, unconscious. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Sirens&nbsp;pierced&nbsp;the<br />air&nbsp;as a patrol car sped to&nbsp;stop a few feet away. Two officers got<br />out and ran over, stopping just in front of Ash and Frank. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Frank's&nbsp;at it<br />again," the first officer said. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Got it bad this time,"<br />the second said, cuffing the unconscious old man. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Hey Ash, you OK? Want to file<br />a report?" The first officer asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash rose to his knees, catching his<br />breath. The throbbing was subsiding now, but he felt it all over. "Maybe<br />later George, I'll let you know. I just need to get out of here," Ash<br />replied as calmly as he could. Inside he was shaking, he needed to get home<br />ASAP. He hoped his look said it all.&nbsp;George was part of his Veterans<br />support group, he knew Ash&nbsp;didn't like crowds and people confrontations.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">George looked down at Ash's jeans,<br />"No problem. You get yourself cleaned up, we need to take Frank in anyway. We'll talk later. Call me when you're<br />up to it." The two officers each grabbed one of Franks' arms&nbsp;and<br />threw him into the back of the squad car. They didn't bother to belt him, body&nbsp;laid awkwardly across the<br />back seat. The patrol car backed up and<br />took off down the street.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Are you OK sir?" a male<br />voice spoke from behind. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash turned slightly, the voice came<br />from the man in glasses. He was wearing a yellow polo, with two overlapping Es,<br />and gray slacks. "I'll be alright," Ash replied reaching for the bump<br />growing on the back of his head.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Let me help you," glasses<br />said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash took his hand, reluctantly.<br />"Thanks," he said. As he rose,&nbsp;one thousand&nbsp;other fights<br />from his past life&nbsp;ached&nbsp;through&nbsp;his bones. He still worked out<br />every day, but 40 was definitely not the new 20. He was glad for the assistance<br />after all.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Oh my,"&nbsp;Glasses<br />said. "Your coffee has spilled all over your jeans. That must have been<br />awful to be attacked like that, let me take you to get another coffee."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">With&nbsp;heart still&nbsp;stampeding through his chest, Ash replied,<br />"Thanks for the offer, Mr.--?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Briar. Dr. Briar actually,"<br />the man replied.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Well thanks for the offer Dr.<br />Briar, but&nbsp;I've had enough excitement for one day. I just need to get<br />home, and cleaned up." <i>And get some Purell.</i><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">"Another time then," Dr.<br />Briar smiled, pudgy flat face pinched a little too tight. Too interested, too<br />helpful. But maybe Ash was over-reacting, considering the incident he just<br />endured. Then again, turtleneck's total lack of personality kept him on the<br />edgy side. Ash's&nbsp;nose felt tight as his brow wrinkled at the thought. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">He&nbsp;waved them off<br />and&nbsp;walked down the street, wanting to get home, his refuge from humanity,<br />as fast as possible. He needed to escape, re-center, and espresso wasn't going<br />to do it this time. He needed a medically induced coma, 6 hours if he was<br />lucky. He turned back one more time to glance at the location of the incident.<br />Glasses was still watching him, turtle neck&nbsp;still behind. A nauseousness<br />crept into his belly, and he turned back toward his truck. Ash kept an eye on<br />window panes and the mirrors on cars he passed, to ensure he wasn't being followed.<br /><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">In the town parking lot, just behind<br />the main buildings,&nbsp;was his escape. The&nbsp;1977 Ford F-150 wore a faded<br />brown paint job, which blended into the rust spots. He adjusted the mirror<br />again, ensuring no one was watching him. The engine turned over like a beast<br />waking from slumber. The smooth floor shifter knob was familiar, and his<br />stomach calmed a bit.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">He made his way down Ashland's main<br />street,&nbsp;a town named after his Great Great Grandfather, so he was told. A<br />few miles down the highway, he turned onto a private road. Taking the driveway<br />two miles into the woods, he parked in front a two story cabin style home.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash glanced back&nbsp;one last time,<br />ensuring nobody followed him. He paused and listened for any sounds out of the<br />ordinary, tires on the gravel, shuffling in the house, a branch breaking.<br />Paranoia satisfied, he inserted the key, opened the door, and flipped the<br />switch.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Office to the left, den to the<br />right, hallway, <i>secure</i>. He made his way down the hall to the kitchen,<br />grabbed an orange bottle from the shelf and twisted the white top. <i>They<br />should call these adult proof</i>. Two blue&nbsp;peacekeepers&nbsp;dropped into his hand. He placed the<br />pills&nbsp;on the counter,&nbsp;took a large plastic Braveheart collectors cup<br />out of the dish drying rack&nbsp;and filled it.&nbsp;The pills washed down<br />nicely with some Dr. Pepper and Jack Daniels, half and half, over ice.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The stairs to the second floor were<br />hard on his knee caps, but he managed with one hand, keeping a tight grip on<br />the cup of sleep. The fight replayed in his mind as&nbsp;Ash walked over to the<br />bookcase and pushed it aside, revealing a large steel Liberty Safe. A few spins<br />of the dial, and the heavy door swung open. He may not be licensed to carry<br />anymore, but that didn't stop him from keeping a stash of weapons, dating back<br />several generations of Ashland's leading on up to his retirement from the<br />United States Air Force, Special Tactics unit. Tonight felt safer with having<br />something nearby, so he grabbed the Glock 9mm, two extra clips, and the holster<br />and&nbsp;stashed them in the kitchen drawer so they'd be handy, just in case.<br />House alarm set, he made his way&nbsp;out onto the patio which was tucked away<br />out of sight. <i>Police report</i>, he<br />remembered and grabbed a sticky note and stuck it to the face<br />of&nbsp;the&nbsp;cell phone.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The hammock swung slightly, creaking<br />as it adjusted to his weight. It enveloped him like a cocoon, shielding him<br />from the events of the day. The first signs of stars peeped&nbsp;through the<br />evening&nbsp;sky. A chorus of crickets and critters chirped, his heart settling<br />into the rhythm of the night as Ash finished off the last of Dr. Braveheart<br />Daniels' magic potion.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The image of&nbsp;the man-bull<br />lingered in his mind, <i>so real, </i>he laid his head down and the<br />image&nbsp;faded to sweet nothingness.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The mess hall wasn't much to look<br />at, a building in the middle of&nbsp;a desert Air Force base, thrown together<br />with gray cinderblocks and a make-shift<br />kitchen. It was over one hundred fifteen degrees outside, but the mess hall<br />managed to keep a balmy ninety-five. The<br />last of what passed for French fries sat on the tray&nbsp;in front of him.<br />"Did you hear what General Akbar said?" two&nbsp;soldiers at the<br />table in front of Ash were talking. "Yeah, another three months of this<br />hell-hole." Ash stood, grabbed the brown plastic tray that doubled as a plate<br />and walked to the trash to dump it.</span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">There was a bright flash, a shockwave&nbsp;propelled him&nbsp;face first<br />onto the wall, all went dark momentarily. When he came to, Ash found<br />himself&nbsp;on the floor of what was left of the mess hall. For a moment Ash<br />was deaf, accept a high pitched frequency ringing in both ears.&nbsp;Vision<br />returning, bracing his hand on the wall, he raised to his feet, the air cleared<br />some but&nbsp;smoke still obscured&nbsp;the scene as he tried to see what<br />happened.</span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Heart&nbsp;racing, hearing<br />normalizing. Nothing felt broken, everything moved. He took a step, nothing<br />hurt. He tried to make out his immediate surroundings. The ceiling was caved<br />in, mostly rubble. "Help me with this!" one soldier barked to<br />another, they lifted several fallen cinder blocks from a fallen soldier's leg.<br />From what Ash could tell from this&nbsp;distance&nbsp;it would need to be<br />amputated. Another concussion came from outside, a cloud of dust and smoke rose<br />to the sky, which was now visible from inside the mess hall. </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash glanced once more at the scene<br />in front of him, there were enough rescuers, he needed to stop the onslaught.<br />He pushed through some fallen wall nearby<br />and paused to listen. A third explosion hit a building on the far side of camp,<br />there didn't seem to be a pattern. </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">An image of the armory, his rifle;<br />he ran down the lane and hooked a left to the Armory. Rifle in hand, Ash took<br />off and found a steel communications<br />tower just higher than the rest of the buildings, and climbed to a small flat<br />spot he could use as a nest. They were targeting large buildings, it was a risk,<br />but an educated risk. </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ash steadied his breathing, after<br />the&nbsp;long run and climb, and positioned himself&nbsp;low on the flat<br />surface of the tower so his profile would be nearly invisible on the horizon.<br />The high powered scope revealed ten men,&nbsp;faces covered by<br />their&nbsp;keffiyehs and robes, hiding behind an outcropping of rocks and three<br />old jeeps. Two of them were holding rocket launchers, two others were helping<br />re-load. The rest were stationed around them with AK-74M Assault Rifles, ready<br />to defend their position.</span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Taking aim at the bulky portion of<br />the first rocket launcher itself, and not its handler, should incapacitate it,<br />and Ash rarely missed. The unit exploded in its handlers face. </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">He looked away. This was his first<br />kill, but his stomach turned a little each time. It's us or them, they'd<br />started this, he would finish it. His blood boiled at the decision. Resolved, he peered<br />back through his scope and found the&nbsp;small band of attackers had turned<br />toward their fallen comrade, surprised by the explosion. The look on their face<br />said that they thought it had malfunctioned.</span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">That gave him the few seconds he<br />needed to line up the second shot. A second explosion took the remaining rocket<br />launcher and its handler out of commission. "Yes," he hissed to<br />himself. The band no longer posed an immediate threat to his buildings from<br />this distance, not with those rifles, but they were still a threat. A threat<br />now reduced to eight with no&nbsp;long range weapons. Three of the men&nbsp;decided to leave the relative safety of the<br />outcropping&nbsp;and run the mile and half of open desert firing at the outer<br />walls of the base.&nbsp;Like ducks flying&nbsp;from a bush.&nbsp;Ash&nbsp;aimed<br />and fired. Seven. Six. Five left. He re-centered<br />his scope on the rocks, the remaining five popped up periodically, firing<br />aimlessly.</span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Whack A Mole, he thought briefly,<br />and a "hmph" escaped his<br />throat. Ash&nbsp;fired&nbsp;each time he found a clean shot. Four. Three. Two.<br />For&nbsp;less than&nbsp;30 seconds, there was stillness from the desert. A<br />shadow by the farthest Jeep. The last two&nbsp;crawled inside, the Jeep sped<br />away from the scene. They were keeping low, out of sight. Ash aimed at the back<br />of each front seat. Two. One. The vehicle drove erratically and crashed into<br />the hill a little further out. The front gates of the base opened as an armored<br />attack vehicle raced out. They'd clean up anything he missed, his job was done.</span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Back on solid ground, he surveyed<br />the scene. Devastation. By the time he'd taken the rocket launchers out, there<br />were four buildings decimated. The dead would be counted after the rescue was<br />over. Despite the sinking feeling in the pit of his stomach that wouldn't<br />leave, he chose to feel no remorse about the job he had to stop the attack. He<br />brushed the feeling aside and ran to back<br />to the mess hall to clean up. </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">As he ran down the street toward the<br />mess hall, the&nbsp;scene turned white and morphed. Now he was jogging down a<br />long hallway with white walls and porcelain tiles. He stopped before a door,<br />tugged on his shirt, and glanced down at the letter he'd received that morning.<br />"Due to valor in the line of fire, you are hereby invited to join an<br />experimental&nbsp;program for soldiers with your unique skill set. Your invitation and participation is optional -&nbsp;classified as Top Secret.<br />Please report for a briefing at 10:00 hours..." Ash glanced at his watch,<br />2 minutes to 10:00. The door plaque read:<br />General Akbar. </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">He raised his hand and knocked.<br />"Come," the voice replied. Ash opened the door, "Permission to enter, Sir?" General Akbar looked up,<br />"Come in soldier." As he<br />entered, General Akbar stood- </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The image morphed again, General Akbar's uniform was now worn by a larger man<br />shaped body, with the head of a bull. White striped streaked across its face. </span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The scene turned to black...</span></i><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The sound of crickets slowly brought<br />Ash back to his cabin patio. The hammock swinging slightly in the&nbsp;mountain<br />breeze through the mountain pass. Another one, <i>ugh</i>. He rubbed his eyes and glanced at his wrist. 3:00 hours.<br />His body ached, his heart was racing but his mind was foggy. Like it hadn't<br />fully kicked on. <i>Why would anyone live in a big city? Ugh... I need to go to town and file that report.<br />Maybe George will come up here instead... </i><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">A single falling star shot across<br />the night sky among the backdrop of a clear milky way. He decided to lay there<br />until his mind rebooted or shut down. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal; mso-margin-bottom-alt: auto; mso-margin-top-alt: auto;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">END Scene 1<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><br /><br /><div class="MsoNormal"><br /><a href="mailto:Me@DarrellWolfe.com">Me@DarrellWolfe.com</a><o:p></o:p></div><br /><br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><br /><div><br /><br /></div><br /></blockquote><br /><h4 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px; margin: 0px; position: relative;"><br />By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><br /><i>Storyteller | Creative | <a href="http://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">INFJ</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;<a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/Purchase/en-US/Product?Path=Clifton%20StrengthsFinder" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Intellection</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;Ideation | Input | Learner | Achiever | Multipotentialite</i></blockquote><br /><ul><br /><li><i>See my full about page and disclosures: <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/what-is-this.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>.&nbsp;</i></li><br /><li><i>Also check out my <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">Resources and Suggestions </a>for writers (<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">here</a>).&nbsp;</i></li><br /><li><i>To Subscribe to the <a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/AuthorFun" target="_blank">RSS: Click Here</a></i></li><br /><li><i>For Non-Fiction writing by <a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe: Click Here</a></i></li><br /></ul><br /><ul><br /></ul><br />
